title: 🎙️ Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life
description: Today we head into the wild — the Chilcotin (chil-KOH-tin) plateau, river valleys, and backcountry that define the Cariboo experience. We're covering the technology helping monitor, protect, and navigate these landscapes: wildfire detection, wildlife tracking, satellite communication, and the tools keeping backcountry travellers safe and conservationists informed. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we head into the wild — the Chilcotin (chil-KOH-tin) plateau, river valleys, and backcountry that define the Cariboo experience. We're covering the technology helping monitor, protect, and navigate these landscapes: wildfire detection, wildlife tracking, satellite communication, and the tools keeping backcountry travellers safe and conservationists informed.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: wildfire detection tech and BC Wildfire monitoring systems, satellite/drone surveillance for Chilcotin wilderness, wildlife tracking and conservation technology, watershed sensors and salmon habitat monitoring, weather stations and climate tracking in backcountry, avalanche forecasting tech, trail mapping and GPS systems, outdoor recreation apps and connectivity, backcountry communication devices (satellite messengers, Meshtastic), flood/drought prediction systems, remote cameras for wildlife research, and how technology helps manage and protect Cariboo wild spaces. Prefer articles that bridge wilderness/conservation AND technology. Penalise heavily (score 0–20): prehistoric and ancient biology (fossils, palaeontology, deep-time creatures) with no contemporary conservation technology angle — these belong to Sunday's Science theme; AI and data centre infrastructure stories about business strategy, investment, or corporate politics rather than direct wilderness, ecosystem, or land-use impact in BC; geopolitical conflict, war, and crime news with no outdoor or conservation relevance; consumer tech reviews and financial market stories; general political and judicial news. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [The Guardian Global Development] ‘It’s open green space versus construction’: Delhi’s historic polo club and the battle for the city’s future
items.content_html:
🌲 ✏️

The government’s requisition of a historic green space has ignited a fierce debate about air quality and heat stress in India’s scorching capital

For decades, the social highlight of winters in Delhi for the “beautiful people” was the polo season. A sprinkling of royalty and diplomats, impeccably groomed women in pearls and chiffon saris, along with wealthy industrialists sporting silk pocket squares used to gather to watch polo players compete under the mild, balmy sun.

They cheered on handsome players who, once the match was over, had children shrieking in delight as they put on a heart-stopping display of tent-pegging derring-do. Swish champagne lunches and other après-polo celebrations followed.

Continue reading...

items.content_html:

items.title: [The Guardian Global Development] Trafficked, beaten and raped: raids reveal scale of abuse of women in Asia’s cyberscam centres
items.content_html:
🌲 ✏️

As tens of thousands are freed, female survivors are increasingly reporting gender-based violence in the compounds, previously thought to hold mainly men

Late one evening in October 2023, Sarah* felt labour pains starting. It was 11pm, but at the cyberscam compound inside Laos’ Golden Triangle, workers were logging on for a long night shift, scamming Americans online.

Every night, workers sat at their computers until the early hours, building fake profiles of glamorous, jet-setting women on Facebook and Instagram. Sarah trawled the web to find older men to target with messages, where she fawned over their jobs, asked how their day had been and exchanged photos of luxury travel and beach trips. Each conversation she had was meticulously designed to follow a multi-day script, and monitored by bosses who walked up and down the long rows of desks.

Continue reading...
items.summary: As tens of thousands are freed, female survivors are increasingly reporting gender-based violence in the compounds, previously thought to hold mainly men Late one evening in October 2023, Sarah* felt labour pains starting. It was 11pm, but at the cyberscam compound inside Laos’ Golden Triangle,

items.title: [CFJC Today Kamloops] NorthPaws win Canada Day battle, drop three-game series against Falcons
items.content_html:
🌲 ✏️

File photo. (Image Credit: CFJC Today) By Aaron Schulze NorthPaws NorthPaws win Canada Day battle, drop three-game series against Falcons Jul 3, 2026 | 6:50 AM Share on Facebook Share on Bluesky Share on X Copy Link KAMLOOPS — The Kamloops NorthPaws dropped their three-game home-and-home-and-home series with the Kelowna Falcons. The NorthPaws were blown out by the Falcons on the road 19-7 Thursday (July 2) to close out the three-game set. It comes after dropping the first game in Kelowna on Tuesday 11-6. Overall, the NorthPaws are 0-4 in Kelowna in the 2026 West Coast League (WCL) season.

(Image Credit: CFJC Today) By Aaron Schulze NorthPaws NorthPaws win Canada Day battle, drop three-game series against Falcons Jul 3, 2026 | 6:50 AM Share on Facebook Share on Bluesky Share on X Copy Link KAMLOOPS — The Kamloops NorthPaws dropped their three-game home-and-home-and-home series with the Kelowna Falcons. The NorthPaws were blown out by the Falcons on the road 19-7 Thursday (July 2) to close out the three-game set. It comes after dropping the first game in Kelowna on Tuesday 11-6. Overall, the NorthPaws are 0-4 in Kelowna in the 2026 West Coast League (WCL) season.

items.title: [Boing Boing] A mystery DVD mailed from Poland hid coded threats in a plague doctor video
items.content_html:
🌲 ✏️

In October 2015, a Swedish tech blogger named Johny Krahbichler opened a package mailed from Warsaw: a DVD with a long alphanumeric code written on it, no return address, no explanation. When he played it, he found a two-minute black-and-white video of a figure in a plague doctor costume, standing in a ruined building, holding up a hand with a blinking light — set to loud, discordant buzzing. — Read the rest

The post A mystery DVD mailed from Poland hid coded threats in a plague doctor video appeared first on Boing Boing.
